encodeField.markdownDescription:
The single field expression each forwarder's output stream writes per event

Selected per forwarder mode by the user-facing config.yaml: - **receive** (default): `fullText` — plain passthrough, no encoding - **compact-all**: `encoded=encode()` — every event compacted - **compact-lookup**: `encoded=shouldEncode() ? encode() : fullText` — per-pattern   decision via the compactReceiver module (requires `compactReceiverLookupFile`)  Advanced users can override directly to customize the output field expression. (Default: fullText)

writeTemplates.markdownDescription:
Whether the forwarder emits new TenXTemplates alongside encoded events

True for encoding modes (compact-all, compact-lookup) so decoders can reconstruct events from templateHash + vars. False for receive mode (no encoding → no templates needed). (Default: false)

dropFilter.markdownDescription:
The filter expression each forwarder output stream uses to decide whether a (possibly regulator-marked) object is written

Resolved once by the user-facing config.yaml from the `outputSoftDrop` flag, so the soft/hard logic lives in one place instead of being duplicated per stream: - **hard** (default): `isObject && !this.isDropped` — regulator-dropped objects are NOT written - **soft** (`outputSoftDrop` truthy): `isObject` — everything is written; the output field compacts the dropped ones  Streams reference it via `$?outputDropFilter` (named so to avoid colliding with the built-in per-stream `outputFilter` option). (Default: isObject && !this.isDropped)
